discrepancies.issue: No shingle specification provided. Cannot verify compliance with minimum 240 lb/sq requirement.
discrepancies.recommendation: Specify GAF Timberline HDZ (Class 4 IR, 130 mph wind rating) or equivalent meeting Texas wind/hail requirements.

discrepancies.issue: No underlayment specification provided. Cannot verify ASTM D226 Type II or ASTM D4869 compliance.
discrepancies.recommendation: Specify synthetic underlayment (ASTM D4869) or felt (ASTM D226 Type II) for entire roof deck.

discrepancies.issue: No ice & water shield specification for 296 linear feet of valleys. Texas code requires minimum 36" width in valleys and eaves.
discrepancies.recommendation: Add 296 LF of ice & water shield for valleys plus eave protection (minimum 36" from wall line).

discrepancies.issue: No ventilation specification provided. With 3,181 sq ft attic space, requires minimum 21.2 sq ft NFA (1:150 ratio).
discrepancies.recommendation: Specify ridge vent system (471 LF ridges) plus soffit vents to achieve 1:150 ratio or balanced 1:300 with proper intake/exhaust.

discrepancies.issue: No flashing material specification for 10 LF flashing and 57 LF step flashing. Cannot verify minimum 26 gauge galvanized steel compliance.
discrepancies.recommendation: Specify 26 gauge galvanized steel or aluminum flashing for all penetrations and wall intersections.

discrepancies.issue: No wind rating specification provided. McKinney requires 115 mph wind resistance per ASCE 7-16.
discrepancies.recommendation: Specify shingles with minimum 115 mph wind rating (preferably 130 mph for insurance discounts).

discrepancies.issue: No impact resistance rating specified. Class 4 IR strongly recommended for Texas hail zones and insurance discounts.
discrepancies.recommendation: Specify Class 4 Impact Resistant shingles (GAF Timberline HDZ, Owens Corning Duration Storm, etc.).

discrepancies.issue: No fastener specification provided. Cannot verify minimum 1-1/4" roofing nail compliance with 4-nail pattern.
discrepancies.recommendation: Specify 1-1/4" minimum roofing nails, 4 per shingle, with 6-nail pattern in high-wind zones.

codeUpgrades.recommendedSpec: GAF Timberline HDZ (Class 4 IR, 130 mph wind rating)
codeUpgrades.justification: Class 4 IR provides superior hail protection, qualifies for 20-35% insurance premium discounts, and offers better wind resistance (130 mph vs 90 mph). ROI typically 3-5 years through insurance savings.

codeUpgrades.recommendedSpec: Synthetic underlayment (ASTM D4869) - GAF FeltBuster or equivalent
codeUpgrades.justification: Synthetic underlayment offers superior tear resistance, longer exposure time during installation, and better moisture protection compared to traditional felt. Critical for Texas weather conditions.

codeUpgrades.recommendedSpec: Ice & water shield in all valleys (296 LF) - GAF WeatherWatch or equivalent
codeUpgrades.justification: Valleys are high-stress areas prone to leaks. Ice & water shield provides waterproof barrier and extends roof lifespan. Required by most manufacturers for warranty compliance.

codeUpgrades.recommendedSpec: Ridge vent (471 LF) + soffit vents for balanced ventilation
codeUpgrades.justification: Proper ventilation reduces attic temperatures by 20-40°F, extends shingle life by 20-30%, prevents ice dams, and reduces cooling costs. Essential for Texas heat.
6-nail pattern significantly improves wind uplift resistance in edge zones where wind forces are highest. Recommended for 115+ mph wind zones.

summary: This EagleView measurement report provides accurate dimensional data but lacks ALL material specifications required for building code compliance. Current compliance score is 45/100 (non-compliant) due to missing shingle specs, underlayment, ice & water shield, ventilation, flashing materials, wind ratings, and fastener specifications. Recommended upgrades to Class 4 IR shingles, synthetic underlayment, enhanced valley protection, and proper ventilation would bring compliance to 95/100 while adding $5,700-$8,200 in material costs—justified by insurance discounts, extended lifespan, and superior storm protection.
